bullet_points: Load the raw dataset and handle missing values ('.').
Identify and print exact column names for QHD, QGM, QKB_1, QKB_2, and QGO blocks.
Encode binary variables (QHD, QGM, QF, QED_1, QKC, QGH) to 0/1.
Map ordinal Likert scales (QEA, QEB, QGP, QGU, QKB_1, QGO) to numeric values.
Create composite indices for Job Security (current/future) and Nature of Work Change (current/future).
Build a unified QGM composite score (sum of 17 binary comfort items).
Create binary 'Usage State' (QA_1_1: Non-user=1, Active=0) and encode QGN (ordinal) and QKD (dummy).
Generate a country-specific ordinal income rank from QDF columns.
Save the cleaned, processed dataframe to a file.
Perform EFA on 16 binary QHD items using a tetrachoric correlation matrix.
Extract "Positive Affect" and "Negative Affect" factors; print factor loadings and variance explained.
Calculate and validate factor scores using McDonald’s omega.
Z-standardize factor scores and append them to the processed dataset file.
Fit LCA models for 2, 3, and 4 classes using current/future job security indices.
Select the best solution based on BIC, AIC, and Entropy; print descriptive statistics (mean job security per class).
Print the full class-conditional response probability table for the selected solution.
Assign class membership to each respondent and save the updated dataset.
Filter dataset for "Resiliently Optimistic" and "Anxiously Declining" classes.
Perform binary logistic regression (1=Resilient, 0=Anxious) using Affective Disposition, HIDDG, QDB, and QDA (random intercept).
Include QKB_1_4 (Regular Training) and QKB_1_11 (Employee Involvement) as predictors.
Report FDR-corrected p-values and odds ratios.
Test the "Dual-Pillar" hypothesis by adding an interaction term (QKB_1_4 * QKB_1_11) to the logistic model.
Perform moderation analysis for "Informed Skeptic" segments by interacting QKB_1_11 with the QGM composite score.
Print Johnson-Neyman interval boundaries and simple slopes at low, mean, and high QGM values.
Generate a heatmap/bar chart of LCA class-conditional item probabilities.
Create a confusion matrix for the logistic regression classifier.
Plot predicted probabilities of class membership across training/involvement levels.
Create a simple slopes plot for the Informed Skeptic moderation and an odds ratio plot with 95% CIs.
Save all plots as high-resolution PNG files.

Structure the Results section with subsections: (1) Sample and Class Descriptives, (2) Affective Disposition Factor Structure, (3) LCA Model Selection and Class Profiles, (4) Main-Effects Logistic Regression, (5) Dual-Pillar Interaction Test, (6) Informed Skeptic Moderation, and (7) Sensitivity Checks.
Synthesize quantitative findings, interpret the "Dual-Pillar" effect, and discuss the role of Affective Disposition.
Ensure complete coverage of all statistical outputs and visualizations generated in previous steps.
